title: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution via Unsafe Java Deserialization in Northwind Gateway
description: The /cluster/sync endpoint deserializes attacker-controlled binary payloads carried in the X-NW-Cluster-State header through a permissive ObjectInputStream with no class allow-list. Because cluster traffic is 'trusted' solely on the presence of a static header, no authentication is required. A gadget chain built from the Commons-Collections variant present on the default classpath yields code execution as the gateway service account. A single crafted POST reproduces the issue reliably against a stock install. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Full compromise of the gateway node, including exfiltration of brokered TLS private keys and upstream service credentials, and lateral movement into every backend the gateway fronts. Observed exploitation path: one request to interactive reverse shell.

title: Authentication Bypass via JWT Algorithm Confusion in Aperture Identity Broker
description: The token verifier trusts the 'alg' value from the attacker-supplied JWT header. By switching a token to HS256 and signing it with the broker's PEM-encoded RSA public key (published at /.well-known/jwks.json) used as the HMAC secret, an attacker forges valid session and administrator assertions — the classic RS256↔HS256 confusion. No prior credentials are needed. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Forgery of arbitrary identities, including tenant administrators, with no corresponding audit event. Every relying party federated to the broker inherits the takeover, collapsing the entire SSO trust chain. Confidentiality and integrity fully compromised; availability unaffected.

title: Server-Side Request Forgery in Cortex Inference Server Model-Fetch Enables Cloud Credential Theft
description: The model loader accepts a 'model_uri' parameter and fetches it server-side with no scheme or host validation, following HTTP redirects. Pointing it at http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/ returns IMDSv1 role credentials, which are reflected back inside the artifact-parse error message. The shipped deployment guide does not require IMDSv2. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Theft of the inference node's IAM role credentials, granting read access to model-artifact buckets and, in tested configurations, the training data lake. The scope change lets an attacker pivot from the application into the cloud control plane. Publicly disclosed; vendor fix pending.

title: Pre-Authentication Blind SQL Injection in Helios Historian Trend Export
description: The /api/trend/export 'tagFilter' parameter is concatenated directly into a T-SQL statement, and the endpoint is reachable pre-authentication on the operator HMI network. Time-based blind injection (WAITFOR DELAY) is confirmed; where the database service runs as 'sa' — common in field deployments — xp_cmdshell is reachable. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Extraction of historian tag data and the operator credentials table, and, where xp_cmdshell is enabled, OS command execution on the historian host — a direct pivot into the OT/ICS environment with safety-relevant consequences. Under active coordinated disclosure; patch in progress.

title: Authenticated OS Command Injection in Meshwork SD-WAN Orchestrator Diagnostics
description: The ping/traceroute diagnostics form interpolates the target host into a shell command as a string. An administrator — or an attacker riding a stolen admin session — injects '; <cmd>' to execute commands as root. The input filter only strips spaces and is bypassed with ${IFS}. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Root code execution on the orchestrator that governs the entire SD-WAN fabric, enabling malicious configuration and route pushes to every managed edge device. Blast radius is fabric-wide despite the PR:H requirement.

title: Unauthenticated Path Traversal / Arbitrary File Read in Northwind Object Store
description: The S3-compatible GetObject handler URL-decodes the object key twice, so a doubly-encoded traversal sequence (%252e%252e%252f) survives normalization and escapes the bucket root. This permits unauthenticated reads of arbitrary files on the node, including configuration, service-account tokens, and objects belonging to adjacent tenants. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Cross-tenant data exposure plus disclosure of the node's own service credentials, which escalates to full-cluster read access. Held under a 90-day coordinated embargo; technical detail and PoC withheld until the vendor ships a fix.

title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting in Aperture Admin Portal Tenant Display Name
description: The tenant 'display name' field is rendered without output encoding in the platform super-admin tenant roster. A low-privileged tenant operator stores a script payload that executes in a super-admin's browser the moment they open the tenant list. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Privilege escalation from tenant operator to platform super-admin via session-riding actions such as minting an admin API key. The scope change carries execution from the tenant context into the parent admin context.

title: Use of Hard-Coded Cryptographic Key in Helios Field SDK Offline Cache
description: The SDK encrypts its offline credential cache with a static AES key compiled into the shipped binary and identical across every install. The key is recoverable in seconds (a plain 'strings' pass over the bundled native library), after which any exfiltrated cache blob — from a device backup or seized hardware — can be decrypted. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Recovery of long-lived field-operator API tokens from device backups or lost/seized devices. The AC:H rating reflects the need to first obtain a cache blob; once obtained, decryption is deterministic. Coordinated disclosure ongoing.

title: Insecure Direct Object Reference Exposes Cross-Tenant Models in Cortex Model Registry
description: GET /api/v1/models/{id}/download authorizes on authentication only, never verifying tenant ownership of the requested object. Model IDs are sequential integers, so any authenticated user can enumerate and download other tenants' proprietary model weights. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: Wholesale theft of other tenants' model intellectual property and any training data embedded in the weights. Publicly disclosed after the vendor exceeded the agreed 90-day window without a remediation.

title: Sensitive Enrollment Token Disclosure in Meshwork Edge Agent Debug Log
description: With log level set to debug, the agent writes the full enrollment bearer token in cleartext to /var/log/meshwork/agent.log, which ships world-readable (mode 0644). Any local low-privileged user can read the token from the log. LoSec sample advisory — fictional product for illustration.
impact: A local user harvests the edge enrollment token and can re-enroll or impersonate the device to the orchestrator. Severity is constrained by the requirement for local access and a non-default debug log level.
